summary: Composites unlimited image layers in a stack with per-layer blend mode, opacity, and adjustment controls. Replaces the typical chain of multiple Composite TOPs for stacked compositing.

key_params.name: numlayers
key_params.note: Number of layer inputs to expose (each becomes layer1, layer2, ...)

key_params.name: layerN_blendmode
key_params.note: Per-layer blend mode (Normal, Add, Multiply, Screen, Overlay, etc.)

key_params.name: layerN_enabled
key_params.note: Toggle a layer on/off without disconnecting it

key_params.name: layerN_adjust
key_params.note: Reveal per-layer adjustment params (brightness, contrast, hue, etc.)

key_params.name: background
key_params.note: Background plate behind the entire stack

common_gotchas: Resolution comes from the first connected layer by default — set `outputresolution` explicitly if downstream nodes assume a fixed size.
Migration: dependent networks that used a chain of Composite TOPs may calculate resolution differently after switching to Layer Mix TOP.
Disabling a layer with the toggle is faster than rewiring — prefer it for live performance.
